topic: Two strangers realize they've been dating the same person
raw_output:
[story 1]

The coffee shop was a blur of steam and chatter, but my focus narrowed to the man across the small table. His familiar, slightly crooked smile, the way he tucked a stray lock of hair behind his ear—it hit me like a physical blow. I stared, my hands suddenly trembling so violently I had to grip the edge of the wooden table to keep from shaking. Then, the other woman at the next table laughed, a sound so perfectly pitched, so utterly *his*, that a hot flush crawled up my neck. I glanced back at him. His eyes met mine, and for one terrifying second, I thought he might know. My breath hitched, shallow and ragged. I felt a sudden, overwhelming need to bolt, to disappear into the crowded street, but my feet were rooted to the spot. The sheer impossibility of the moment made my vision swim.

[story 2]

"Wait, you're wearing the blue scarf," I managed to choke out, my voice sounding unnaturally high. Across the cafe, a stranger looked up, and the recognition that sparked in his eyes was immediate and devastating. It was the look I’d seen a thousand times, the one that used to make my chest ache with a strange, giddy panic. I dropped my pastry, the crumbs scattering onto the floor, and I didn't bother to clean them up. My palms were slick, clammy against the ceramic mug in my hand. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the tile, and I started walking, not toward the exit, but toward him, a desperate, clumsy lurch in my step, needing to confirm the impossible truth with my own eyes.

[story 3]

The ambient noise of the gallery opening seemed to amplify the frantic drumming in my ears. I was watching him across the room—the way he tilted his head when he listened, the slight furrow in his brow when he concentrated. It was a blueprint I’d memorized. When his gaze swept the room and snagged on mine, a jolt shot through me, sharp and blinding. My mouth went dry, and I felt a sudden, desperate urge to laugh, but no sound came out; only a strange, tight knot formed in my throat. I took a stumbling step forward, my mind racing through every shared inside joke, every late-night conversation, trying to reconcile the reality of this shared space with the sheer, unbelievable coincidence unfolding before me. The world tilted slightly on its axis.
